1	# Flux
2	
3	Umbrella app for Workouts, Meditate, and Check-in. Ships as a web PWA at
4	[flux.gunk.dev](https://flux.gunk.dev) and as a single Android APK with three
5	launcher icons (one per tab).
6	
7	See `docs/UMBRELLA-PLAN.md` for the plan and `docs/VISION.md` / `docs/DESIGN.md`
8	for product intent.
9	
10	## Stack
11	
12	- Vite + Preact + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS
13	- Capacitor for the native Android shell (`@capacitor/{core,cli,android,app,haptics}`)
14	- Playwright for e2e tests
15	- No router, no state library, no UI component library
16	
17	## Development
18	
19	All commands run inside `nix develop` (nix is mandatory per `CLAUDE.md`):
20	
21	```bash
22	nix develop --command npm ci          # install deps
23	nix develop --command npm run dev     # Vite dev server on :3030
24	nix develop --command npm run build   # production build to dist/
25	```
26	
27	E2E tests use a separate shell that ships Playwright browsers:
28	
29	```bash
30	nix develop .#test --command npx playwright test
31	```
32	
33	## Android
34	
35	The `android` dev shell provides JDK 17, gradle, and the Android SDK
36	(platform 34, build-tools 34.0.0). It pulls multi-GB unfree SDK components,
37	so it is intentionally separate from the default shell:
38	
39	```bash
40	nix develop .#android --command npm ci
41	nix develop .#android --command bash -c "npm run build && npx cap sync android"
42	nix develop .#android --command bash -c "cd android && ./gradlew assembleDebug"
43	```
44	
45	The debug APK lands at `android/app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk`.
46	
47	CI builds the APK via `setup-java` + `setup-android` in
48	`.github/workflows/test.yml` rather than this nix shell — the toolchains are
49	decoupled intentionally.
50	
51	## Local Android install
52	
53	Build and install the debug APK on a connected device (USB or Android Studio
54	wireless debugging):
55	
56	```
57	nix develop .#android --command ./scripts/local-install.sh
58	```
59	
60	The script: `npm install` → `vite build` → `cap sync` → `./gradlew assembleDebug` →
61	`adb install -r`. First gradle run pulls AGP + AndroidX deps (a few minutes);
62	subsequent runs are fast. If install fails with a signature mismatch (a
63	previously-installed CI build has a different debug keystore), uninstall the
64	app (`adb uninstall <appId>`, where `<appId>` is the `appId` from
65	`capacitor.config.ts` — currently `dev.gunk.flux`) and re-run. Your IndexedDB
66	data will be wiped, so export a backup first via Settings → Data → Export if
67	it matters.
68	
69	## Project layout
70	
71	```
72	├── index.html              # Vite entry
73	├── src/
74	│   ├── main.tsx            # Preact entry; reads window.AppEntry bridge
75	│   ├── App.tsx             # Tab shell
76	│   ├── tabs/               # Workouts, Meditate, Checkin, Settings
77	│   ├── llm/                # Provider-agnostic generate() interface
78	│   └── db/                 # Per-tab IndexedDB stores (added in feature PRs)
79	├── android/                # Capacitor-scaffolded Android project
80	├── tests/smoke.spec.ts     # Playwright smoke test
81	└── capacitor.config.ts
82	```

file.content:
# Flux

Umbrella app for Workouts, Meditate, and Check-in. Ships as a web PWA at
[flux.gunk.dev](https://flux.gunk.dev) and as a single Android APK with three
launcher icons (one per tab).

See `docs/UMBRELLA-PLAN.md` for the plan and `docs/VISION.md` / `docs/DESIGN.md`
for product intent.

## Stack

- Vite + Preact + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS
- Capacitor for the native Android shell (`@capacitor/{core,cli,android,app,haptics}`)
- Playwright for e2e tests
- No router, no state library, no UI component library

## Development

All commands run inside `nix develop` (nix is mandatory per `CLAUDE.md`):

```bash
nix develop --command npm ci          # install deps
nix develop --command npm run dev     # Vite dev server on :3030
nix develop --command npm run build   # production build to dist/
```

E2E tests use a separate shell that ships Playwright browsers:

```bash
nix develop .#test --command npx playwright test
```

## Android

The `android` dev shell provides JDK 17, gradle, and the Android SDK
(platform 34, build-tools 34.0.0). It pulls multi-GB unfree SDK components,
so it is intentionally separate from the default shell:

```bash
nix develop .#android --command npm ci
nix develop .#android --command bash -c "npm run build && npx cap sync android"
nix develop .#android --command bash -c "cd android && ./gradlew assembleDebug"
```

The debug APK lands at `android/app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk`.

CI builds the APK via `setup-java` + `setup-android` in
`.github/workflows/test.yml` rather than this nix shell — the toolchains are
decoupled intentionally.

## Local Android install

Build and install the debug APK on a connected device (USB or Android Studio
wireless debugging):

```
nix develop .#android --command ./scripts/local-install.sh
```

The script: `npm install` → `vite build` → `cap sync` → `./gradlew assembleDebug` →
`adb install -r`. First gradle run pulls AGP + AndroidX deps (a few minutes);
subsequent runs are fast. If install fails with a signature mismatch (a
previously-installed CI build has a different debug keystore), uninstall the
app (`adb uninstall <appId>`, where `<appId>` is the `appId` from
`capacitor.config.ts` — currently `dev.gunk.flux`) and re-run. Your IndexedDB
data will be wiped, so export a backup first via Settings → Data → Export if
it matters.

## Project layout

```
├── index.html              # Vite entry
├── src/
│   ├── main.tsx            # Preact entry; reads window.AppEntry bridge
│   ├── App.tsx             # Tab shell
│   ├── tabs/               # Workouts, Meditate, Checkin, Settings
│   ├── llm/                # Provider-agnostic generate() interface
│   └── db/                 # Per-tab IndexedDB stores (added in feature PRs)
├── android/                # Capacitor-scaffolded Android project
├── tests/smoke.spec.ts     # Playwright smoke test
└── capacitor.config.ts
```

content.text: Now I'll create the .envrc, bin/flux-install, and update .gitignore and README.

content.content: File created successfully at: /tmp/klaus-sessions/flux/20260521-0648-cfdddbd5/.envrc (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Wrapper that invokes scripts/local-install.sh inside the .#android
# nix dev shell. PATH_add'd via .envrc so this is available as 'flux-install'
# anywhere inside the flux repo.
set -euo pipefail
REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
exec nix develop "${REPO_ROOT}#android" --command "${REPO_ROOT}/scripts/local-install.sh" "$@"

The script: `npm install` → `vite build` → `cap sync` → `./gradlew assembleDebug` →
`adb install -r`. First gradle run pulls AGP + AndroidX deps (a few minutes);

input.new_string:
The script: `npm install` → `vite build` → `cap sync` → `./gradlew assembleDebug` →
`adb install -r`. First gradle run pulls AGP + AndroidX deps (a few minutes);
subsequent runs are fast. If install fails with a signature mismatch (a
previously-installed CI build has a different debug keystore), uninstall the
app (`adb uninstall <appId>`, where `<appId>` is the `appId` from
`capacitor.config.ts` — currently `dev.gunk.flux`) and re-run. Your IndexedDB
data will be wiped, so export a backup first via Settings → Data → Export if
it matters.

**With direnv:** the repo includes a `.envrc` that auto-loads the default dev
shell on `cd`. The first time you enter the directory after pulling this
change, run `direnv allow` once. From then on, you can run `flux-install`
directly from anywhere inside the repo — it's a wrapper that invokes the
android dev shell and runs `scripts/local-install.sh` for you.

Umbrella app for Workouts, Meditate, and Check-in. Ships as a web PWA at
[flux.gunk.dev](https://flux.gunk.dev) and as a single Android APK with three
launcher icons (one per tab).

See `docs/UMBRELLA-PLAN.md` for the plan and `docs/VISION.md` / `docs/DESIGN.md`
for product intent.

## Stack

- Vite + Preact + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS
- Capacitor for the native Android shell (`@capacitor/{core,cli,android,app,haptics}`)
- Playwright for e2e tests
- No router, no state library, no UI component library

## Development

All commands run inside `nix develop` (nix is mandatory per `CLAUDE.md`):

```bash
nix develop --command npm ci          # install deps
nix develop --command npm run dev     # Vite dev server on :3030
nix develop --command npm run build   # production build to dist/
```

E2E tests use a separate shell that ships Playwright browsers:

```bash
nix develop .#test --command npx playwright test
```

## Android

The `android` dev shell provides JDK 17, gradle, and the Android SDK
(platform 34, build-tools 34.0.0). It pulls multi-GB unfree SDK components,
so it is intentionally separate from the default shell:

```bash
nix develop .#android --command npm ci
nix develop .#android --command bash -c "npm run build && npx cap sync android"
nix develop .#android --command bash -c "cd android && ./gradlew assembleDebug"
```

The debug APK lands at `android/app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk`.

CI builds the APK via `setup-java` + `setup-android` in

## Local Android install

previously-installed CI build has a different debug keystore), uninstall the

data will be wiped, so export a backup first via Settings → Data → Export if

```

├── src/

│   └── db/                 # Per-tab IndexedDB stores (added in feature PRs)
├── android/                # Capacitor-scaffolded Android project
├── tests/smoke.spec.ts     # Playwright smoke test
└── capacitor.config.ts
```

@@ -66,6 +66,12 @@ app (`adb uninstall <appId>`, where `<appId>` is the `appId` from
 data will be wiped, so export a backup first via Settings → Data → Export if
 it matters.
 
+**With direnv:** the repo includes a `.envrc` that auto-loads the default dev
+shell on `cd`. The first time you enter the directory after pulling this
+change, run `direnv allow` once. From then on, you can run `flux-install`
+directly from anywhere inside the repo — it's a wrapper that invokes the
+android dev shell and runs `scripts/local-install.sh` for you.
+
 ## Project layout
 
 ```
diff --git a/bin/flux-install b/bin/flux-install
new file mode 100755
index 0000000..d87260f
--- /dev/null
+++ b/bin/flux-install
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# Wrapper that invokes scripts/local-install.sh inside the .#android
+# nix dev shell. PATH_add'd via .envrc so this is available as 'flux-install'
+# anywhere inside the flux repo.
+set -euo pipefail
+REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
+exec nix develop "${REPO_ROOT}#android" --command "${REPO_ROOT}/scripts/local-install.sh" "$@"
 ```

content.content:
{"body":"## Summary

Adds `scripts/local-install.sh` — a single-command helper that runs
`npm ci` → `vite build` → `npx cap sync android` → `./gradlew assembleDebug` →
`adb install -r` against whatever device adb currently sees (USB or
Android Studio wireless debugging).

Usage:

```
nix develop .#android --command ./scripts/local-install.sh
```

The script bails early if `ANDROID_HOME` isn't set (i.e. run outside the
`.#android` shell), and prints a recovery hint with `adb uninstall dev.gunk.flux`
when install fails with a signature mismatch against a previously-installed CI
build. README has a matching "Local Android install" section.

This is purely dev-experience tooling — no production or CI behavior change.
CI's `build-android` job in `test.yml` is untouched, and the script lives
outside `package.json` scripts on purpose so it's clear it needs the Nix shell.

## Test plan

- [x] `bash -n scripts/local-install.sh` passes
- [x] `shellcheck scripts/local-install.sh` is clean
- [x] Executable bit committed (`100755` in the tree)
- [x] `klaus _pre-review` clean

Run: 20260519-1520-7327b58c","title":"scripts: add local-install.sh for one-step build + adb install"}

input.command:
gh pr edit 80 --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Adds `scripts/local-install.sh` — a single-command helper that runs
`npm ci` → `vite build` → `npx cap sync android` → `./gradlew assembleDebug` →
`adb install -r` against whatever device adb currently sees (USB or
Android Studio wireless debugging).

Usage:

```
nix develop .#android --command ./scripts/local-install.sh
```

**With direnv:** the repo now ships a `.envrc` (`use flake` + `PATH_add bin`)
and a `bin/flux-install` wrapper. After `direnv allow` on first entry, you can
just run `flux-install` from anywhere inside the repo — it invokes the
`.#android` dev shell and runs `scripts/local-install.sh` for you. The default
`.envrc` shell is intentionally the light dev shell (not `.#android`), so a
bare `cd` doesn't force the multi-GB Android SDK fetch on web-only work.

The script bails early if `ANDROID_HOME` isn't set (i.e. run outside the
`.#android` shell), and prints a recovery hint with `adb uninstall dev.gunk.flux`
when install fails with a signature mismatch against a previously-installed CI
build. README has a matching "Local Android install" section.

This is purely dev-experience tooling — no production or CI behavior change.
CI's `build-android` job in `test.yml` is untouched, and the script lives
outside `package.json` scripts on purpose so it's clear it needs the Nix shell.

## Test plan

- [x] `bash -n scripts/local-install.sh` passes
- [x] `shellcheck scripts/local-install.sh` is clean
- [x] `bash -n bin/flux-install` passes
- [x] `shellcheck bin/flux-install` is clean
- [x] Executable bit committed (`100755` in the tree) for both scripts
- [x] `.direnv/` added to `.gitignore`
- [x] `klaus _pre-review` clean

Run: 20260519-1520-7327b58c
EOF
)"

content.text:
Done. Pushed commit `c6a3299` to PR #80 with:

- `.envrc` (2 lines: `use flake` + `PATH_add bin`)
- `bin/flux-install` — exec wrapper that runs `scripts/local-install.sh` inside `.#android` shell, committed with `100755` mode
- `.gitignore` — added `.direnv/` near top (grouped with `node_modules/` / `dist/`)
- `README.md` — added the "With direnv" paragraph to the "Local Android install" section
- PR description updated to mention the direnv shortcut and the intentional light-default rationale

Validation: `bash -n` and `shellcheck` both clean on `bin/flux-install`.
